summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened a message on building godly relationships, specifically addressing marriage. He began by challenging the common idea of a 'match made in heaven' as a pre-ordained perfect person, explaining that a strong marriage is actually built through intentional work on earth, grounded in God's principles. The foundational scripture was Psalm 127:1, emphasizing that unless the Lord builds the house, the builders labor in vain. Pastor Travis acknowledged the reality of divorce statistics but proclaimed that a foundation built on Jesus Christ transcends worldly odds. He established the biblical definition of marriage from Genesis 2:24, where two become one flesh, and love from 1 Corinthians 13:4-8, which describes God's perfect, never-failing love. The sermon focused on two practical ways to build a 'match made in heaven': first, to keep the romance alive through deliberate affection and dating one's spouse, referencing Proverbs 5:18-19. This led to a biblical discussion on sex, affirming it as God's sacred design for marriage (Hebrews 13:4) and contrasting it with the world's shifting standards. Pastor Travis addressed the topic of abortion from a biblical perspective, citing Psalm 139:13-16 to affirm God as the giver of life, while offering grace and hope to those who have experienced it. The second point focused on cultivating Christ-like communication, identifying it as the core issue in most relationship struggles. He taught from James 1:19 to be quick to listen, slow to speak, and slow to become angry, and from Colossians 3:19 for husbands to love their wives and not be harsh. Using proverbs about the power of the tongue (Proverbs 18:21) and the destructiveness of nagging (Proverbs 21:19, 27:15-16), he emphasized speaking life, taking responsibility, and listening with empathy instead of being easily offended. The message concluded with a call to surrender to God, trusting in Jehovah Jireh as our provider, and an invitation to salvation.
mainPoints: A 'match made in heaven' is built on earth through intentional work, with God as the foundation (Psalm 127:1).
Keeping romance alive involves deliberate affection, dating your spouse, and honoring God's design for sex within marriage.
Christ-like communication—being quick to listen, slow to speak, and slow to anger—is essential for healthy relationships.

versesMentioned.text: Unless the LORD builds the house, the builders labor in vain. Unless the LORD watches over the city, the guards stand watch in vain.
That is why a man leaves his father and mother and is united to his wife, and they become one flesh.

Love is patient, love is kind. It does not envy, it does not boast, it is not proud. It does not dishonor others, it is not self-seeking, it is not easily angered, it keeps no record of wrongs. Love does not delight in evil but rejoices with the truth. It always protects, always trusts, always hopes, always perseveres. Love never fails.
May your fountain be blessed, and may you rejoice in the wife of your youth. A loving doe, a graceful deer— may her breasts satisfy you always, may you ever be intoxicated with her love.
Marriage should be honored by all, and the marriage bed kept pure, for God will judge the adulterer and all the sexually immoral.

For you created my inmost being; you knit me together in my mother’s womb. I praise you because I am fearfully and wonderfully made; your works are wonderful, I know that full well. My frame was not hidden from you when I was made in the secret place, when I was woven together in the depths of the earth. Your eyes saw my unformed body; all the days ordained for me were written in your book before one of them came to be.

The husband should fulfill his marital duty to his wife, and likewise the wife to her husband. The wife does not have authority over her own body but yields it to her husband. In the same way, the husband does not have authority over his own body but yields it to his wife. Do not deprive each other except perhaps by mutual consent and for a time, so that you may devote yourselves to prayer. Then come together again so that Satan will not tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
The tongue has the power of life and death, and those who love it will eat its fruit.
My dear brothers and sisters, take note of this: Everyone should be quick to listen, slow to speak and slow to become angry.
Husbands, love your wives and do not be harsh with them.
Better to live in a desert than with a quarrelsome and nagging wife.
A quarrelsome wife is like the dripping of a leaky roof in a rainstorm; restraining her is like restraining the wind or grasping oil with the hand.

Monday.scripture: Psalm 127:1 (NIV) - 'Unless the LORD builds the house, the builders labor in vain.'
Monday.action: Spend 5 minutes in prayer, asking God to be the foundation of your key relationships today.
Monday.prayer: Lord, I surrender my relationships to You. Build my house, so my labor is not in vain.

Tuesday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 13:4-5 (NIV) - 'Love is patient, love is kind. It does not envy, it does not boast, it is not proud.'
Tuesday.action: Practice one specific attribute of love from 1 Corinthians 13 with someone close to you.
Tuesday.prayer: God, fill me with Your perfect love today. Help me to be patient and kind in my words and actions.

Wednesday.scripture: Proverbs 5:18 (NIV) - 'May your fountain be blessed, and may you rejoice in the wife of your youth.'
Wednesday.action: Do one intentional act of affection or appreciation for your spouse or a family member.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, show me how to delight in and cherish the people You have placed in my life.

Thursday.scripture: James 1:19 (NIV) - 'Everyone should be quick to listen, slow to speak and slow to become angry.'
Thursday.action: In one conversation today, focus entirely on listening without planning your response.
Thursday.prayer: Holy Spirit, make me quick to listen, slow to speak, and slow to anger. Guide my conversations.

Friday.scripture: Colossians 3:19 (NIV) - 'Husbands, love your wives and do not be harsh with them.'
Friday.action: Monitor your tone and words today. Commit to speaking gently, without harshness or nagging.
Friday.prayer: Lord, guard my tongue. Let my words build up and bring life, not tear down or cause pain.

Saturday.scripture: Psalm 139:13-14 (NIV) - 'For you created my inmost being; you knit me together in my mother’s womb. I praise you because I am fearfully and wonderfully made.'
Saturday.action: Reflect on the value God places on every life. Perform an act of kindness for someone who needs encouragement.
Saturday.prayer: Jehovah Jireh, my Provider, thank You for the gift of life. Help me to see and value others as You do.
